title: Run baseline feedback evaluation with RCA
description:
As a score optimizer
I want to run a baseline feedback evaluation with root-cause analysis
So that I can establish a starting point and understand current error patterns

## Acceptance Criteria

- Baseline evaluation runs successfully with feedback data
- RCA output includes topic-clustered analysis of errors
- Baseline metrics captured (AC1, Accuracy, Precision, Recall)
- Evaluation ID stored for future comparisons

## Gherkin Specification

```gherkin
Feature: Baseline Feedback Evaluation with RCA
  As a score optimizer
  I want to run a baseline feedback evaluation
  So that I can establish starting metrics and error patterns

Scenario: Run baseline evaluation on score with feedback data
  Given a scorecard "customer-service" with score "empathy"
  And feedback data exists for the past 90 days
  And the score has a champion version
  When I run the feedback alignment optimizer
  Then a feedback evaluation is created with evaluation_type="feedback"
  And the evaluation completes with status="COMPLETED"
  And baseline metrics are captured:
    | Metric    | Present |
    | AC1       | Yes     |
    | Accuracy  | Yes     |
    | Precision | Yes     |
    | Recall    | Yes     |
  And root-cause analysis output contains clustered topics
  And the baseline evaluation ID is stored for future comparisons

Scenario: Handle insufficient feedback data gracefully
  Given a scorecard "test-scorecard" with score "new-score"
  And no feedback data exists for the past 90 days
  When I run the feedback alignment optimizer
  Then the procedure fails with a clear error message
  And the error message indicates "insufficient feedback data"
  And no evaluation record is created
```
